topic: Program 5 (Craft/Home/Mobile) at 0% interest up to OMR 20K — who qualifies and how to apply
format: Open with a question about a business idea the reader has been sitting on, then show the exact first step to fund it
post:
Up to OMR 20,000 at 0% interest exists specifically for home bakeries, mobile services, and small craft workshops — and most people who qualify never apply.

The program is called Program 5. Development Bank Oman created it for exactly these kinds of businesses: craft, home-based, mobile. Up to OMR 20,000, zero interest, no equity handed over.

Where most people get stuck is the feasibility study. You need one before you can apply, and a lot of applicants either skip it or pay OMR 150–300 for something that gets rejected anyway. Not because they're doing something wrong exactly — but because the study wasn't structured the way DBO and Riyada actually review it. Financials, market sizing, projected cash flow, sector fit. Miss those or present them loosely, and the application stalls.

The study doesn't need to be elaborate. It needs to be right.

One thing worth fixing before you even get there: if your Sanad office management is a WhatsApp group and a spreadsheet, sort that out first. Reviewers notice when documentation is clean. It signals that you're running a real operation, not just an idea.

So the sequence is simple. Get the feasibility done properly. The funding becomes a lot more realistic from there.

If you want a starting point, there's a free tool that builds a bank-ready feasibility study in about 10 minutes — it covers all 10 Development Bank Oman / Riyada programs: https://www.thesmartpro.io/feasibility-studio?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=feasibility

What's actually stopping you from submitting today?
